topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the house had become a heavy, suffocating blanket. Every time Leo retreated to his room, I felt a prickle on the back of my neck, a constant, low-level hum of unease. It started subtly—a change in his phone habits, a sudden aversion to being near it, and then, the discovery. I found the login details tucked inside a forgotten charging cable. My hands trembled slightly as I navigated the unfamiliar interface. The pictures weren't of school events or friends; they were curated snapshots of an existence I hadn't known existed. A cold knot tightened in my stomach. I stood there for a long time, the glow of the screen reflecting in my wide, unblinking eyes, processing the sheer breadth of what my son was hiding.

[story 2]

I watched Maya pace in front of the hallway mirror, her shoulders tense, her gaze darting toward the closed door of her bedroom. She kept fiddling with the edge of her hoodie, a nervous habit I recognized instantly. The air in the living room felt charged, brittle. When I finally asked about her phone, her response was too quick, too bright. There was a flicker in her eyes, a momentary hesitation before she deflected. I noticed the way her fingers gripped the edge of the sofa cushion, white-knuckled. A strange, unwelcome certainty settled over me; something was deliberately obscured. I knew, with a certainty that settled deep in my chest, that there was a secret world operating just beyond my sight.

[story 3]

It was the late-night scrolling that finally cracked the facade. I was tidying up the study, a mundane task, when I found the password written on a sticky note hidden beneath a textbook. My breath hitched. The content that flooded my screen was a dizzying kaleidoscope of carefully filtered moments—parties I didn't know about, laughter that felt rehearsed, a carefully constructed persona. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscles strain beneath my skin. I backed away slowly, my movements stiff, as if afraid the very air might betray me. The quiet of the house suddenly amplified, every creak of the floorboards sounding like a spotlight on the deception I had just uncovered.
